# Professional Experience

```{=typst}
#resume-entry(title: [Lecturer and Research Associate in Statistics & Methods], location: [Lucerne, Switzerland], date: [08/2025 -- present], description: [University of Lucerne, Switzerland (20% appointment)])
#resume-item[
- Building and developing the statistics and research methods curriculum in the B.Sc. Psychology program
- Designing, teaching, and examining one statistics lecture with accompanying exercises per semester
- Developing interactive teaching and exercise materials for hands-on statistical methods training in R
- Contributing to the curricular design of the methods track
]
#resume-entry(title: [SNF Ambizione Fellow], location: [Zurich, Switzerland], date: [01/2023 -- present], description: [University of Zurich (UZH), Switzerland])
#resume-item[
- 100% appointment until 07/2025, 80% from 08/2025
- Junior group leader, research group "Cognitive Psychometrics"
- Leading an independent research group in psychological methods, statistics, and psychometrics
- Developing and applying predictive analysis and modeling approaches using hierarchical Bayesian statistics
- Designing and implementing an open-source software package for Bayesian measurement models (R package bmm)
- Responsible for budget, personnel planning, and scientific direction of an SNSF-funded research project (CHF 950,000, 4 years)
- Planning, conducting, and analyzing experimental studies (lab and online)
- Teaching advanced statistical methods
]
#resume-entry(title: [Postdoctoral Researcher], location: [Zurich, Switzerland], date: [01/2019 -- 12/2022], description: [University of Zurich (UZH), Switzerland])
#resume-item[
- Senior researcher in the "Cognitive Psychology" research group at UZH
- Contributing to and leading international research projects at the intersection of psychometrics and cognitive psychology
- Publishing findings in international peer-reviewed journals and presenting at international conferences
- Securing and contributing to externally funded projects
- Teaching courses and supervising bachelor and master theses
- Coordinating and serving as point of contact for methodological and technical questions in science computing
]
#resume-entry(title: [Ph.D. Student], location: [Heidelberg, Germany], date: [09/2015 -- 12/2018], description: [Heidelberg University, Germany])
#resume-item[
- Junior researcher in the "Differential Psychology & Diagnostics" research group
- Independently conducting a research project on individual differences in intelligence
- Applying advanced statistical and psychometric modeling techniques
- Teaching programming and experimental methods
- Continuing training in cognitive modeling methods
]
#resume-entry(title: [Research Assistant], location: [Heidelberg, Germany], date: [09/2010 -- 08/2015], description: [Heidelberg University, Germany])

]
```

# Third-Party Funding

```{=typst}
#resume-entry(title: [The Binding Hypothesis -- A Unified Account of Cognitive Individual Differences], location: [SNF (CHF 916'510)], date: [2023--2026], description: [PI | competitive])
#resume-item[
- This project investigates individual differences in cognitive processes by combining experimental paradigms with formal measurement and process models. The aim is to develop theoretically grounded indicators of cognitive processes and examine their relationship with higher-order performance measures. The project includes leading an independent research group with full budget and personnel responsibility.
]
#resume-entry(title: [Flexibility Grant], location: [SNF (CHF 120'000)], date: [2023--2026], description: [PI | non-competitive])
#resume-item[
- Supplementary funding within the Ambizione project to support the compatibility of family life and academic work.
]
#resume-entry(title: [Neurocognitive Psychometrics (research network)], location: [DFG (€ 44,645)], date: [2022--2026], description: [Co-PI | competitive])
#resume-item[
- An international scientific network to foster exchange between psychometrics, cognitive psychology, and neurocognition. My role included co-coordinating the network (Co-PI) and contributing to its conceptual direction and grant applications.
]
#resume-entry(title: [Bayes Factor Estimation for Complex Hierarchical Models], location: [Graduate Campus UZH (CHF 4'000)], date: [2024], description: [Co-PI | competitive])
#resume-item[

]
```
